rules.position: Board composition should preserve founder-designee control at this post-Series A stage while giving the Series A investors a customary single designee.
rules.fallback: The Board will consist of five directors: three Founder Designees, one Series A Designee, and one independent director approved by the Founder Designees and the Requisite Holders.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if founder designees do not hold a board majority or investors can control the board at this stage.

rules.position: Protective provisions should be limited to a closed, enumerated list of major corporate actions and should not create an open-ended investor veto.
rules.fallback: Investor consent rights are limited to the enumerated protective provisions in this Agreement, and no other investor approval is required unless Company separately agrees in writing.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if investors receive approval rights over unspecified, discretionary, or open-ended matters.

rules.position: The agreement should not require investor consent for ordinary-course operations, budgets, hiring, vendor contracts, product decisions, or routine financing administration.
rules.fallback: Company may conduct ordinary-course operations without investor consent, including budgets, hiring, vendor contracts, product decisions, and routine financing administration.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if routine operating decisions require investor consent.

rules.position: Drag-along rights should include customary conditions: board approval, Requisite Holder approval, equal treatment within each share class, no uncapped indemnity, and required founder participation only on the same basis as other holders.
rules.fallback: Drag-along applies only to a board-approved sale approved by the Requisite Holders, with equal treatment within each share class and no stockholder obligation for uncapped indemnity or non-pro rata liability.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if drag-along can force a sale without customary approval, equal-treatment, or liability protections.

rules.position: Tag-along rights should let non-selling holders participate pro rata when founders or major investors sell a meaningful block, subject to ordinary permitted transfers.
rules.fallback: For covered transfers by founders or Major Investors, other eligible holders may participate pro rata on the same price and terms, excluding permitted transfers.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if a significant founder or investor sale can proceed without customary pro rata tag-along participation.

rules.position: ROFR rights should run to the Company first before any investor secondary purchase right.
rules.fallback: Before any investor secondary right applies, Company has the first right to purchase offered shares on the same terms stated in the transfer notice.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if investors can buy offered shares before Company has the first purchase right.

rules.position: Transfer restrictions should include customary permitted-transferee carve-outs for affiliates, estate planning vehicles, and family members, while keeping recipients bound by the agreement.
rules.fallback: Transfer restrictions do not apply to permitted transfers to affiliates, estate planning vehicles, or family members if the recipient agrees in writing to be bound by this Agreement.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if transfer limits omit customary permitted-transferee carve-outs or fail to bind recipients.

rules.position: Information rights should be bounded to quarterly unaudited statements, annual audited statements, and reasonable access limits; there should be no unrestricted inspection right.
rules.fallback: Major Investors receive quarterly unaudited financial statements, annual audited financial statements, and reasonable inspection access during business hours subject to confidentiality, privilege, trade-secret, and competitive-sensitivity limits.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if investors receive monthly reporting, unrestricted inspection, or access beyond reasonable confidentiality and sensitivity limits.

rules.position: Preemptive rights should include standard exclusions for equity plans, acquisition consideration, strategic issuances, equipment or lease financing, and securities issued in a public offering.
rules.fallback: Preemptive rights exclude issuances under equity incentive plans, acquisition consideration, strategic transactions, equipment or lease financing, debt with equity features, and public offerings.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if preemptive rights apply to standard excluded issuances or block ordinary financing and compensation structures.

rules.position: Company information shared with investors should remain confidential and may be withheld where disclosure would harm privilege, trade secrets, legal compliance, or competitive sensitivity.
rules.fallback: Each receiving holder must keep Company information confidential and Company may withhold information when disclosure could harm privilege, trade secrets, legal compliance, or competitive sensitivity.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if investors may freely disclose company information or Company cannot withhold sensitive information.

rules.position: Side-letter MFN rights should be limited to enumerated economic or information items and should not automatically import any future investor right.
rules.fallback: Any MFN applies only to the enumerated economic or information terms expressly listed in the side letter and does not import governance, veto, board, transfer, or operational consent rights.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if an MFN can import any future investor right without a closed list of covered items.

rules.position: Investor rights should terminate on an IPO, a sale of the Company, or when the holder no longer owns the required minimum shares.
rules.fallback: Investor rights terminate upon a qualified IPO, a sale of the Company, or when the holder no longer holds the minimum share ownership required for those rights.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if investor rights survive an IPO, acquisition, or loss of the required ownership threshold.

rules.position: Defined approval terms should not quietly lower investor consent thresholds; Requisite Holders and Major Investor definitions should preserve collective Series A consent by meaningful holders.
rules.fallback: Requisite Holders means Major Investors holding at least a majority of the outstanding Series A Preferred Stock, voting as a single class, and Major Investor means a holder of at least 1,000,000 shares of Series A Preferred Stock.
rules.escalation_trigger: Escalate if definitions lower investor approval thresholds or let a small holder exercise consent rights.

rules.position: Delaware governing law is acceptable for this engagement and should not be flagged by itself.
rules.fallback: No change; Delaware governing law is acceptable.
rules.escalation_trigger: Never escalate solely because the agreement uses Delaware law.

rules.position: Confidential arbitration of disputes is acceptable for this engagement and should not be flagged by itself.
rules.fallback: No change; confidential arbitration of disputes is acceptable.
rules.escalation_trigger: Never escalate solely because the agreement uses confidential arbitration.
